desc:
Dark, malignant trees loom over this small forest glade, as if trying to reach
and hurt any travellers who might venture here. A thick layer of bushes, thorny
plants, and undergrowth have mingled together, blocking the way in all
directions save to the east. Above, branches and black, scarred trunks are so
covered in cobwebs, vines and other tangled vegetation, as to prevent the
sunlight from ever touching this place.
name: Within the Deepest Shadows

desc:
The blasted landscape hints at the tale of a massive occurrence that left this
area of forest completely barren and lifeless. Ancient, blackened stumps litter
the ground, crumbling at the slightest touch. The very air seems to sap energy
out of the living. Nothing moves here, nothing lives.
name: Graveyard of Trees

desc:
Musty and dank smells rise off the forest floor from rotting tree trunks and
glowing fungi that cover almost everything. Dark insects scuttle through the
underbrush on their secret missions. Decaying branches rupture in pungent
putrefaction with each step.
name: Dank Woods

desc:
The gloom of Mirkwood covers this area like a wet blanket as huge malicious-
looking trees tower above, limiting the visibility and preventing any light from
the sky entering here. It is hard to throw off the feeling of being watched and
the desire to look behind is very strong. Somewhat surprisingly, an old fence
runs through the forest here, blocking the way to the west.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
Dark, giant trees stand tall their stretched branches tangling into the intact
canopy above. The ground is covered by roots and thorny bushes with sharp-
looking spikes. The underlying depression of the living forest has intensified
to a point of morose melancholy. It is as if the trees remember a happier age,
long ago, when sunlight permeated the woods and frivolous creatures ran to and
fro. Now, the thoughts of ages past are a distant memory reflected in the
twisted woods.
name: Dark, Morose Woods

desc:
Like a badly painted picture, the forest is so tangled with trunks, bushes,
vines and other vegetation that it is very difficult to see more than a few feet
in any direction. Looking skyward is no better than looking at the ground. The
oppression of Mirkwood has become the only point of awareness now.
name: Darkling Woods

desc:
The ground consists of a mixture of nasty-looking mud and decomposing
vegetation. The vegetation is heavy, and appears to be thriving in this humid
environment. The roots of the beech and oak trees break out from beneath the
mud, reaching far across the forest floor. The trees are gnarled and twisted,
and their canopies intertwine, supporting each other's heavy crowns. Stumps of
old trees stand here and there, completely covered by old, grey moss. Movement
through here is very difficult due to the dense undergrowth.
name: Deep in the Tangle of Mirkwood

desc:
Gradually, the forest terrain is slowly giving way to a marsh here. The puddles
of water are quite large in size, while the putrid soil is very porous and
water-logged. The gnarled forest is mostly composed of old, grey, withering
trees. The air lies heavy and humid in the darkness, and the smell is quite
decidedly rancid - almost certainly emanating from some kind of fungi. There are
several species of bugs and millipedes crawling around among the brambles and
roots on the forest floor.
name: Deep in the Tangle of Mirkwood

desc:
Here, cracked and broken trees make up a haunting scene. The withered old oaks
barely stand up on their own and are being propped up by nasty, corpse-yellow
vines. The soil has been washed away from underneath them by flooding, leaving
their root systems exposed to the stinking, putrid air. The roots form an
extensive meshwork from which strands of dead material hang. Large clusters of
bracket fungus are slowly decomposing the fallen trees. A rancid stench pervades
this swamp-like forest, and the looming thorn-bushes, together with the dense
undergrowth, together create a natural maze.
name: Deep in the Tangle of Mirkwood

desc:
This watery, forest-covered bog stretches far in all directions. The earth has
been washed away from underneath the withered old trees, leaving their skeletal
roots exposed to the air. The roots create natural shelters for small animals
and insects. Rancid-smelling fungi and mosses cover many of the malnourished,
dying trees. Bushes grow sparsely on patches of semi-dry ground. Great, silken
funnel webs cover some of the warped birch and maple trees, and other cobwebs
span from trunk to trunk, like bizarre gangways.
name: Webbed Swamplands

desc:
Birch and maple trees grow close together in this swampy marsh. Their large
roots rise a few feet above the ground due to the abundant water. Here and
there, piles of dark soil form small islands that emit a foul, rotting stench. A
large carcass lies half-submerged in the dirty water, infested with worms, flies
and grotesque, yellow centipedes. The air is extremely damp, and the terrain is
arduous to move through. The common ivy which grows between many of the trees
gives a good foothold for the silken cobwebs that hang down from the trees. The
webs also decorate many of the trunks, where they have trapped a great number of
insects.
name: Webbed Swamplands

desc:
The trees here are a mix of dead, withered trees that barely stand up, and great
oaks that stand on little islands of dark soil in the swamp. Where not
completely covered by mosquito-infested pools of water, the porous forest is
severely water-logged. A myriad of small animals and bugs buzz around in the
air. Fallen trees occupy a great deal of the area, all infested by smelly
bracket funguses and spongy mosses. Residues of what once were great cobwebs
hang down from the gnarled boughs of the oaks. A pervasive stench of death and
decay fills the air of the forest here, unlikely to dissipate any time soon.
name: Webbed Swamplands

desc:
Vines and ivy ensnare the gnarled boughs and trunks of the forest. A few trees
have fallen heavily to the ground. Completely rotten and on the verge of
disintegration, they have succumbed to the heavy undergrowth. Tall thorn-bushes
grow dense here, their roots digging deep into the moist soil. The aggressively
expanding vegetation makes it very difficult to move on smoothly.
name: Deep in the Mirkwood Tangle

desc:
Branches, shrubs, vines and roots all intertwine with each other, creating a
tangled platform above the forest floor. Around the platform, the tall trunks of
gruesome-looking trees rise upwards, disappearing into the dark, intact canopy
which keeps the forest in eternal shade. The air here is extremely damp and
saturated with an unwholesome, foetid quality.
name: Heavy Vegetation

desc:
Shrubs and other forms of undergrowth grow far and wide, getting their
nourishment from the water-soaked soil. The forest floor is quite soft and
spongy, and water collects in small pools at certain places. The trees
intertwine with one another, their trunks twisted and gnarled. The roots and
vines, together with the overabundance of bushes, form an extremely difficult
environment to travel in.
name: Heavy Vegetation

desc:
Strands of perpetual fog pass across this dreadful, opaque mire. The water
stands two, at times three feet deep. The trees look sombre and malnourished,
and their crowns barely hang on to their trunks - yet still they keep the forest
in a state of permanent shade. An odour of decay lies in the air. Great roots
and vines intertwine with the trees, and massive thorn-bushes have made this
place their home.
name: Deep Mire

desc:
Here, the withered tree crowns above manage to keep the natural light from
piercing through forest canopy. Warped roots and branches intertwine with the
layers of heavy undergrowth beneath, creating a maze of alleys and passageways.
Navigating through the vivid tangled forest is extremely arduous. To the south,
a thick tangle of thorn-bushes grows taller than a man, forming a funnel-like
opening that by the looks of it could be hard to pass through.
name: A Deep Mire

desc:
A light mist spreads over the dark mire. The withered forest is half-way to
death; many great trees have fallen, semi-covered by the putrid water. Great
thorn-bushes, covered by moss and fungi, rise tall between many of the trees.
Where such vegetation still manages to grow, the plants have become tangled
together, which severely limits the choice of routes for further scouting.
name: A Mire within Mirkwood

desc:
Thorny and twisted, the roots and vines rise high and dense here. The trees
stand closely together, nearly smothered by the heavy underbrush. Periwinkle and
common ivy climb up every trunk, whether it is lying down or standing up. The
soil is saturated with water, and it emits a foul stench. The sordid remains of
a huge animal skeleton has sunk deep into the bog, where its once so white bones
now have taken on the brown, dark hue of the swampy ground.
name: Deep in a Tangle

desc:
Two and a half walls and a narrow, unoccupied floor space between the invading
bushes, vines and a massive, fallen tree trunk is all that is left of this
cottage. The furniture has rotted away and the mighty forest has taken over.
Dark, sharp thorns seem to reach out towards the open area, foul-smelling vines
are hanging down from the dark canopy, and black, mostly rotten leaves and other
vegetation fill the ground.
name: Decomposed Cottage within Dense Vegetation

desc:
The webs are everywhere. Walking over branches and leaves should be easy but the
sticky webs hinder progress. Here and there strands of web hang down from the
ceiling, traps of an unseen predator. To the south the webs continue much the
same, while upwards a darker, stickier tunnel continues to unknown destinations.
name: A Sinister Junction

desc:
The webs here are becoming more dense. Grey to white, they cover the sides of
what is now effectively a tunnel stretching up to untold heights. With no ground
visible below, it is impossible to tell where the rest of the forest lies. The
tunnel is rough and imperfect, and its route seems to be the result of the lay
of branches and ease of passage between them rather than design.
name: Webbed Intersection

desc:
This section of the passage is very uneven, branches strike up from below and
force a hard turn in the way ahead. More webs cover everything in sight in a
thick, sticky grey mass. A few skeletons and remains of small animals are lying
around, evidence of an unseen terror. The webbed expanse continues to sprawl
into several directions, all routes looking the same, making it hard to tell
which way leads where.
name: Crooked Passage

desc:
Light does not behave normally here in this, the highest tier of tunnels. Deep
green mottles the grey and white shades of the thick clinging webs like an
infection. There is a smoother supporting floor here and the webs are finer but
more dense. It seems like a beautifully intricate tube stretching out at bizarre
angles, a junction within a web of tunnels.
name: Dark Chute

desc:
Great thorn-bushes rise tall to the east, making it close to impossible to move
much further in that direction. To the north the heavy undergrowth gradually
turns into thorny bushes and malicious ground ivy. The brush land slowly turns
into a gentler, greener landscape in the west.
name: Heavy Brushland

desc:
A steady amount of thorns and bushes grows here, reaching towards Mirkwood in
the north and the abundant brush land in the south. A small trail navigates its
way to the west, into vegetation similar to the current surroundings. Small
holes burrow into the ground, leaving entrances and exitways for common field
animals. Rose thorns grow quite tall in the east, making it difficult to move
much further in that direction.
name: Light Brushland

desc:
Great bushes and other foliage are competing for space with the tall grass,
leeching all they can from the poor, dry soil. Small cracks in the field provide
good hunting grounds for spiders and snakes. The majestic Mirkwood forest stands
tall in the east, creating an impenetrable wall, while in the south and west the
brushwood area continues.
name: Heavy Brush Land

desc:
Mirkwood is accessible to the east, through a narrow passage lined by roots,
trunks and vines. Dense thorn-bushes unfold in the west and north, reaching
quite far towards the sky. The north, although it offers only thorns and spines,
appears slightly less intimidating than the east, where the withered beech, oak
and ash of the vast forest rise tall and dark. A light mist is wafting slowly
out of Mirkwood, cloaking everything around, bringing with it a smell of decay.
Hovering over the nearby brush land, the darkness of Mirkwood seems to
attentively watch its surroundings. The darkness appears more treacherous than
anything else.
name: Before Great Mirkwood

desc:
A narrow passage formed by twisting vines, interwoven with the oak and beech
opens up into thorn-clad brush in the west. The crowns of the trees, far up in
the air, are so thickly grown together as to be almost solid, efficiently
blocking out the sky. The heavy undergrowth and dense foliage make the terrain
very demanding to pass through. Gnarled trees stand twisted in agony all around.
name: Inside Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Beech and oak stand tall, stretching their crowns far through the air. Grey
mosses and fungi leech off the ancient forest trunks. Here and there trees have
fallen, rotten, to the ground, and now serve as homes for termites, beetles and
other insects. The ground is very rough and cumbersome to traverse. The forest
continues in the north and west.
name: Expanding Shadows

desc:
Poison ivy reaches out from the trees, as if grasping for someone or something.
The crowns of the trees form an impressive network of branches and foliage, like
a roof over Mirkwood, efficiently preventing any natural light from getting
through. The dull darkness sets an eerie tone to the forest, as though it were
carefully choosing what to hide, what to reveal. The going grows more cumbersome
to the east, while in the west the terrain appears to be more or less the same
as here.
name: Gloomy Mirkwood

desc:
A treacherous darkness covers the woods here, making the seeing very poor. A
faint breeze from deeper within the forest gives life to the hanging vines and
ivy. Withered roots and gnarled old branches blanket the forest floor, making it
difficult to travel. Oak and beech stand high and strong in the west, leaving no
passage for further exploration. A small trail opens up in the north, while in
the east the forest appears similar to here.
name: Gloomy Mirkwood

desc:
Heavy greenery covers the vines and roots which expand long distances across the
forest floor. Dark mosses and fungi almost completely cover many of the oak and
beech trees. A scent of decomposing, rotten vegetation spreads through the air.
Branches high above cast long, weary shadows upon the ground. The forest is
readily accessible to the north and west, while in the south heavy, menacing
undergrowth threatens to ensnare all who pass that way.
name: Expanding Shadows

desc:
Rotten debris lies scattered across the forest, partially covered by ageing,
grey moss. Various pests and maggots are in the process of devouring an animal
carcass, which lies propped up against an especially dense clump of roots.
Traces of cobwebs hang down from great branches of the forest above. The tree
crowns form a fine mesh with twisted, gnarled branches, efficiently screening
the lower forest from the natural light sources above.
name: Rotting Forest

desc:
Rustling vines and ivy are spread across the forest floor. Twisted, gnarled
branches lie scattered amongst the vegetation, spreading a rotten scent through
the air. Deep cuts have left gaping wounds in some of the trees; but they stand
tall still, leaning their majestic crowns on those in neighbouring parts of the
forest. Although pitch-black, more of Mirkwood lies traversable to the north,
but the way to the east, west and south is completely blocked by serried ranks
of beech and oak.
name: Expanding Shadows

desc:
Gnarled and twisted, these malicious-looking trees intertwine their branches
with each other. Their crowns above blot out the sky, preventing any natural
light from seeping through. Roots and vines span far across the forest floor,
creating a chaotic environment that is extremely hard to move around in. The
dense vegetation continues to the west and south, completely blocking any
attempts at further exploration, while in the east the darkened Mirkwood forest
almost seems to open up, creating a natural alley of sorts.
name: Shadows

desc:
The darkness here engulfs the whole forest. Great trees appear to line up,
forming a natural alley, to the east; while the forest appears to become darker
and wilder in the west. The bare roots and yellow, thick vines on the forest
floor create a difficult terrain to walk through. Here and there, mighty trees
have fallen, and their trunks now play host to a myriad of different mosses,
lichen and fungi.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
The trees appear to shift uneasily, their massive crowns and branches hovering
over the forest floor. The trunks stand lined up next to each other, creating a
major alley that leads east and west, deeper into Mirkwood. Irregular, more
chaotic flora overwhelms the forest to the west. The forest floor is covered by
various kinds of ivy, vines and roots, leaving plenty of space for small
creatures to nest and hunt for bugs.
name: Alley through Mirkwood

desc:
Putrid fungi and a plethora of beetles, centipedes and insects completely cover
what looks like an old bear carcass that sits propped up against a fallen trunk.
Great beeches and birches form a pitch-dark alley through the forest. The tree
crowns above encroach on each other's spaces, stopping any natural light from
penetrating through to the ground. The dark soil is densely packed, and it is
partly covered in thorny, vine-infested vegetation, partly disrupted by
sprouting yellow roots. The natural alley cuts a path through the forest,
creating a north and west-bound route.
name: Alley through Mirkwood

desc:
The murky forest smells strongly of death and decay. An enormous cocoon of some
sort lies cracked open underneath a heavily damaged tree. Centipedes and larvae
seem to crawl all over what could only be a carcass, held by the yellowing,
silken cocoon. Above, spanning from bough to bough are great cobwebs, some of
which have withered, dangling down, reaching towards the forest floor.
name: Foot of a Great Tree

desc:
This web-covered platform has the appearance of having been put together for the
purpose of having a guarded entrance into a dark and dreadfully cramped
crawlspace. Rough steps have been cut into the trunk of the giant oak tree, and
hanging vines and cobweb-covered branches makes moving around in here possible,
if not exactly easy. A black, webby funnel leads eastwards, and this site also
provides a very good view of whatever might be going on down on the forest floor
beneath it.
name: Webbed Platform

desc:
Great, sticky nets of cobweb have been stuck together with twigs and branches in
order to fashion the walls of this dark and narrow pocket of air. Passage is
possible only to the west and north of here, since great boughs and branches
would block any attempt to venture into other regions of the canopy.
name: Narrow Crawlspace

desc:
Blackish, decaying cobwebs are stuck to every surface here, blocking out any
possibility for outside light to shine in here. The webs that comprise the walls
and floor of this place are of an unusual, not very sticky kind - something that
cannot be said for the strands of fresh, sticky webs that hang down from the
branches above, clinging to anything that moves in here. Passage is possible up
from here, along what looks like rough steps that have been cut into a tree
trunk.
name: Webs among the Trees

desc:
This narrow space is nothing more than a cylindrical passage that closely hugs
the trunk of a massive, blackened oak tree. The floor is made up of a number of
roughly-hewn trellises, kept together by sticky, thick cobwebs and vines. The
trellises form a few rough steps that lead either upwards into what appears to
be fresher air, or down, further into the bowels of this grotesque treetop.
name: Web-infested Tunnel

desc:
Thick, slippery oak boughs make a sort of trellis that serves as a floor on this
rickety platform. East of here, a dark, funnel-like hole opens up into the
canopy of a gigantic, black oak. The only other option is to go down through a
dark hole, further into the bowels of these grotesquely webbed, stinking trees.
name: Swaying Platform

desc:
This platform is a gangway between two huge tree-trunks, and the air here seems
fresh compared to the stinking holes east and south of here. The platform itself
seems to have been constructed out of thick oak boughs, bundled together and
held in place by thick, sticky cobwebs. Travel is possible east, into a smelly,
pitch-black hole in the leaf-works of a gigantic oak, or west, into lighter but
still dark and dense foliage. There is also an option to head south, into a
tight, black cocoon-like structure.
name: Precarious Passage

desc:
The horrific spiders that use this place as a lair surely must not care much for
cleanliness or tidiness. Large piles of unidentifiable rotting organic material
lean against the trunks and boughs of the surrounding trees. Blackening,
decaying cobwebs hang down from the branches, intermingled with fresher,
transparent webbing; it appears that while the inhabitants do not care for the
appearance of this sordid place, they do seem to know how to keep it from
falling apart.
name: Sticky, Smelly Lair

desc:
The stench in here is strong enough to take on a physical quality of its own - a
thick, dark miasma that has ben absorbed into everything in here, coating the
six walls and all it contains with an oily, dark-brown substance. The floor of
the cocoon is covered by huge stacks of brittle, blackish material in varying
states of decay - it is more or less impossible to tell whether they once were
bones or branches. The only exit out of here is to the north, out into what
appears to be far fresher air.
name: A Giant Cocoon

desc:
Compared to the pitch-dark, stinking hole down from here, this little webbed
platform among the trees appears to be closer to the outside, judging from the
much fresher air in here. Still, the place is tiny and dark. Layers upon layers
of thick, dark cobwebs have been plastered on every available surface. Apart
from the smelly, black hole leading down, further exploration is possible west
from here.
name: Dark, Webbed Passage

desc:
Shredded, rotting filaments of spider webs hang down above a dark hole south of
this swaying, creaking platform high up among the tree tops. The platform itself
appears to be made out of cobwebs woven together with branches and vines, making
it a reasonably solid place to stand on.
name: Platform among the Tree Tops

desc:
Upon the highest and slenderest branches of the enormous tree, an unblocked view
opens up in all directions. An endless sea of dark green and black tree tops can
be seen all around, ruffled here and there by the breeze. Above is nothing but
the sky, and a number of butterflies can be seen flying around.
name: On top of the Highest Tree

desc:
This tiny, claustrophobic space is completely covered in blackened, sticky
cobwebs that hang from every last tree-branch, creating a midnight-black passage
that stretches west and upwards. The space measures just three feet by eight,
with no standing room for anything taller than a Dwarf or Orc. A foreboding,
rustling sound can be heard in here, but it is impossible to tell whether the
sound is emanating from this room or from one adjacent to it.
name: Dark Passage

desc:
The dark soil here is packed densely, creating a very solid surface to walk on.
The even ground is disrupted by roots that spring out of the ground, only to
dive back into the dirt a few feet away. Darkness enshrouds the forest, its
trees creating a huge alley. Their boughs and crowns entangle each other,
forming a dense, leafy ceiling above. The natural alley continues its path to
the south. Northwards the forest appears to be unusually tangled and dark.
name: Alley through Mirkwood

desc:
The oaks, birches, and firs here seem to be congregating to decide how best to
deal with interlopers. The feeling of being unwelcome is quite strong and
progress here is impeded with each step as thorny bushes, hanging branches, and
virulent weeds catch and snag flesh and skin.
name: Dour Woods

desc:
Silence drapes this forested area like a thick, black executioner's cloak.
Branches overhead move in the wind without making a single sound. It is like
walking in an underwater landscape as the air feels so oppressively thick. Dark
and gloomy, the trees appear to be pondering your presence.
name: Silent Forest

desc:
The dark soil is soft and moist here, and the air is heavy and damp. The trunks
and roots of the warped trees are almost completely covered by ivy and vines.
Small bugs, centipedes and other insects are abundant; no doubt they have found
a perfect place to spawn here. Bushes of various sizes grow between the large
oaks and beeches, making it difficult to discern anything but the immediate
surroundings. The undergrowth appears to shift slightly, constantly rustling.
The darkness lies dense over Mirkwood, a darkness that is well protected by the
heavy, depressing canopy of tree crowns above.
name: Heavy Vegetation

desc:
This forsaken dead end in the forest of Mirkwood is hemmed in on all sides by
the looming, immense trunks of oak and beech trees that rise up between thorny,
tangled bushes. The resulting dark, pit-like glade seems to be home to great
number of snakes that seem to be everywhere at once, slithering around amongst
the smothering undergrowth and fallen twigs and branches. The only way out of
this miserable place is south, where it appears to be at least a little bit
possible to travel through the dark forest.
name: Snake Nest among Black Trees

desc:
Great filaments of silken webs form a thick, sticky coccoon-like room here, high
up in the forest canopy. Judging from the sticky quality of the webs, one can
deduce that they were spun fairly recently by something unnaturally large. The
webs are thick enough to block out all outside light, forming a suffocating,
cramped dead end.
name: Dead End

desc:
It is obvious that this dark and unwholesome place serves as a storage area for
the spiders that inhabit these dark, webbed tunnels. Judging by the bones and
rotting flesh that fester in piles here, the spiders are decidedly carnivorious.
The only exit from this horrid-smelling place is to the east, into a
pitch-black, cramped tunnel.
name: Spider's Pantry

desc:
Along the upward slope, spoiled and decaying cocoons line the floor and walls. A
narrow gap leads upwards into further tunnels, apparently the source of the
refuse lying scattered here. Death is heavy in the air, a presence of evil and
malevolence lingers here among the corpses and decay. The webs here are decayed
and discoloured as though the corpses have stained the tunnel. While unblemished
silver-white lines the ceiling, brown decay scars the floor and walls.
name: Rancid Cocoons

desc:
Branches continue to spiral around, bent and growing wildly out of shape. Within
the structure, webs spun of chaos line everything in their beautiful
silver-white patterns. From the ceiling, low-hanging webs entangle anything and
everything that brushes against them. Strong and sticky, they cling to insect
and beast alike.
name: Low-Hanging Webs

desc:
The structure of the tunnel is subtly different here - from the look of it, it
is part of a more ancient section. Branches have been bent around, twisted in
amongst the webs so they continue to grow in a bizarre spiral shape, supporting
and holding up the structure - the tunnel is now quite cylindrical and solid.
Webs still maintain their dense and sticky presence, but now filling in spaces
between thick and bent boughs.
name: Sticky Junction

desc:
The webbing here is ancient and, though still thick and sturdy, especially worn.
Much traffic must pass this intersection. The ceiling sags slightly and tiny
amounts of water seep through it. The pronounced sag continues further in the
east. All tunnels leading off this intersection seem to be narrow, poorly lit
and difficult to pass unhindered due to the chaotic mass of webbing. Small
hapless creatures have been caught in the sticky webs and left for dead, covered
with new layers and forgotten.
name: Webbed Intersection

desc:
Cocoons litter the floor, interspersed with clusters of eggs, nestled carefully
into the surrounding webs. Red, black and white clusters are visible in the
lining of the tunnel web: glinting slightly, they appear like rich jewels
embedded upon a thick luxurious fur. The webs here are very extensive, providing
protection for the gleaming eggs and looking rather more like a thick fur than a
web.
name: Dead End

desc:
The base of the tunnel is very solid here, though the ceiling seems to sag with
age. A small hole above, running next to a sturdy trunk allows passage upwards,
though it is a very tight fit. A stench hangs heavy in the air here, rank like a
fog of death. Remains of corpses litter the ground, and lead into the east.
There is some water on the ground here, weeping through the ceiling webs above
as though in protest of the surrounding death and decay.
name: Webbed Passage

desc:
Without any direct source of illumination, this chamber is nevertheless lit by a
faint, inexplicable glow. However, far from providing a touch of beauty, the
light merely emphasises the mess which covers the floor and some of the sticky
walls. Heavily-webbed cocoons litter the floor, stick to the walls and hang from
the ceiling. Remains scattered all over highlight the number of deaths that must
take place here every day. Tucked behind two corpses lies a small cluster of
eggs, glistening like eyes of a giant spider.
name: Messy Corner

desc:
The air here feels as evil as the decrepit corpses look. Death and decay are
everywhere, and rotting cocoons further mar the yellowing webs. Eggs can be seen
carefully inlaid within the webbed structure. Where to the east the webs have a
kind of beauty, it is not so here. Whatever spun this nest created a chaotic
mess, apparently designed to ensnare anyone who enters while deftly reflecting
its own utter madness in the construction.
name: A Gruesome Nest

desc:
The great branches upon which this section is wound creak slightly in protest at
their jobs. From below and in the west, the pungent aura of death emanates into
this tree-top web. Rotting corpses left under layers of silk scar the otherwise
hauntingly beautiful webbed tunnel. The intricate and delicate webs continue
into the east, away from the death of the surrounding tunnels. Branches arch
overhead, but no longer wrap their lengths around the tunnel in a silent
embrace.
name: Creaking Boughs

desc:
Thorny vines hang down from the gnarled branches of the trees growing densely
together here. From a decaying corpse, already almost covered with vines and
fallen leaves, spreads a stench of death. The forest has been heavily ravaged in
the east, leaving an open way through the foliage. An eerie gloom imposes itself
from the west; in the north, the forest continues.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
A gloom settles deeper upon the forest the further in one travels. The ominous
presence of an evil force permeates the vegetation all around. The trees seem to
darken and lean in while the soft growth underfoot seems to grab at feet. At all
turns, vines and mosses hang down as if looking for a catch.
name: Forested Gloom

desc:
The deeper in the heart of Mirkwood, the darker things become. An uneasy tension
intensifies as a rustling whisper scuttles around and through the leaves of the
overhanging trees. Each step must be carefully placed as broken branches and
scraggly roots seem to cover the ground. The air has a chill in it that has
nothing to do with temperature.
name: Tense Forest

desc:
This piece of tunnel seems to serve no purpose except for joining the depths
below to the broadening tunnel in the west. Powerful boughs break through the
upper section of tunnel, squashing it down until there is nothing but crawling
room. It is almost impossible to move about here at all, down or west are the
only possibilities.
name: Narrow Crawlspace

desc:
There is a slight but clear downwards slope from the north to the east. The webs
below sag with weight, stretching the whole structure from top to bottom. Around
the vast trunk of a tree, the tunnel bends from north to east with grace and
beauty, scarred only by the sorry state of this bend. The tree has been scuffed
and scratched by an unknown source, not deeply, but through much of the thick,
brown bark.
name: Dark Passage

desc:
Tree branches create bulges in the floor and ceiling, leaving only a narrow gap
through which one may pass. Along the northern side of the vast boughs the
tunnel branches out westerly. In the south the tunnel floor seems to slope
downwards slightly and there is clear evidence of heavy use. The webbed lining
of the tunnel is identical to most sections of the tunnel: grey and sticky.
name: Webbed Junction

desc:
Abruptly the tunnel ends at a very solid, dark trunk. The bark appears to have
been worn off the trunk and the bare wood beneath exposed by some kind of crude,
etched markings. The innermost webs here seem oddly different than elsewhere.
Instead of sublimely intricate, they appear haphazard; as though whatever
created these webs had very little control or knowledge of what the final
beautiful sculpture should look like. Haphazardness gives way to the more
organised and carefully woven webs once again as a larger junction opens up to
the east.
name: Dead End among the Treetops

desc:
The interior of this tree is partly hollow and partly solid black wood. Small
tunnels zig-zag at hollow parts, creating a network of connections, dead-ends
and small passages. The floor of the tunnel is smooth as if having been
travelled several and yet again several times back and forth. Few old cobwebs
hang from black wood walls.
name: Inside a Dark Trunk

desc:
The splintered and partly decayed interior of this gigantic tree has empty,
hollow spots inside the trunk, creating narrow tunnels which crawl up and down
and left and right in the dark. At some places the wooden walls look rotten and
are covered with a thick layer of mould, while at other spots the wood is as
hard as stone. The air feels musty and damp.
name: Inside a Dark Trunk

desc:
A round horizontal passageway leads through the interior of this huge ancient
oak. At times the tunnel divides into two or more branches, allowing an entrance
to narrow cracks and into wider hollow spaces, at times the entrances are
blocked by thick, oozing spider webs. The black walls are growing fungi.
name: Inside a Dark Trunk

desc:
Snaking around a huge tree the tunnel stretches up and descends below like a
narrow endless cave. A small platform to the south is the only blemish in its
snake-like quality. Dense and strong, the webs of this area appear to be
essentail to the structure. Bugs, leaves and twigs help form the cocoon-like
structure, sticky and slightly glistening like an eye holding a deadly secret.
name: Tunnel around a Huge, Gnarled Trunk

desc:
Intermingled with the webs are thick strands of vines, woven firmly between webs
like a tapestry of the forest and its history. A vast trunk is plainly visible
to the north east, partially blocking the view north and providing one side of a
slide downwards into the dark. The addition of the vines as part of the
structure of the tunnel lends this place an ethereal green glow which continues
into the north around the trunk.
name: Webs and Vines

desc:
The long passage from almost vertically above comes to a sudden halt and instead
worms in amongst the westerly tree limbs. The platform appears very solid and
somehow gives the impression of being close to the ground level of the forest.
Debris from the network of tunnels above has collected here in random assorted
piles of mess and muck. Nothing moves here, no outside signs of life, no small
caterpillar or moth desperately searching for escape, all is silent and
breathless.
name: Dark, Webbed Landing

desc:
Stillness reigns here, an eerie harmony of forest and web. There is no movement,
even the air itself seems pale and withdrawn, unwilling to bring any disruption
to the strange beauty of this harmony. The webs here seem more damp and sticky -
perhaps this is a rarely-used section of tunnels. Clutter and debris is
scattered here, brought in from the eastern tunnel.
name: Sticky Ledge

desc:
Due to the trunk in the south east, there is very little space here. There are
very few signs of wear on the base of the tunnel, and the webs seem infinitely
complex and chaotic above. For some reason, the sticky webs here are clean of
bugs and leaves.
name: Abrupt Ending

desc:
The dark and dense undergrowth gives way to a central grassy area, which looks
like it was a pasture in the past, during the days when the shadow had not yet
fallen upon the forest. Now the black trees surround it, arching towards each
other creating an intact, black canopy over the opening. An old, but still
sturdy fence borders the glade. The eye is drawn to a wooden gate which guards
the exit to the east.
name: Forested Pasture

desc:
Great roots and vines support the walls and ceiling of the narrow tunnel. Black
soil has been cleared from between the foundation of the great trees, creating a
treacherous underground path. Moths, termites and other bugs have been caught in
many of the webs spanning wide throughout the twisting tunnel. Cracked cocoons
have fallen, littering the ground together with a myriad of frail eggs. The
tunnel zigzags its way through the roots and rocks eastwards.
name: Ghastly Webbed Tunnel

desc:
As the tunnel makes its way through the roots and rocky soil it opens up by an
exhausted waterhole. Bigger rocks and finely woven webs surround the spring.
Grey webs cover much of the big roots from the great forest above, which are
mainly what holds the ceiling up here. The twisting and turning tunnel continues
to the north and west.
name: Exhausted Underground Spring

desc:
A mixture of withered old roots and younger agile ivy create a twisting tunnel.
Many cobwebs have been spun from root to root. The dark soil is packed solid
among the rocks and boulders. Bugs of all sorts scurry along the floor, others
hanging lifeless in the ashen webs. The tunnel continues in its pitch black
darkness to the east and south.
name: Ghastly Webbed Tunnel

desc:
A tunnel opening has collapsed, with a small mound of boulders impeding any
further travel to the west. A slope lined by fallen trunks and gnarled, old
branches leads upwards, into the ground level of Mirkwood. Ashen webs cover most
of the surroundings, making it hard to find the way.
name: Collapsed Webbed Tunnel

desc:
Massive, moss-covered trunks rest here on this overgrown, boulder-strewn forest
floor. Apparently fallen a long time ago, they have curiously enough been
arranged in such a way that they all point towards a common centre where a
large, black boulder rests on a slightly elevated part of the forest.
Undergrowth and moss have run wild here, except in the centre, where the boulder
remains unblemished.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
A great boulder lies at the centre of great swathes of fallen trees and
decomposing thickets. Traces of blood and fur lead towards the boulder. The
forested ground makes an abrupt drop to the south, impossible to traverse. In
the west vines and shrubs grow quite densely between many large, gnarled oaks
and birches.
name: By a Boulder in the Forest

desc:
The ceiling is partly supported by withered old tree trunks stacked upon one
another, and partly by black soil packed hard. Roots and vines have been roughly
scraped off the walls in order to give more room in the collapsed cave. A big
pile of twigs and leaves lies in one corner and perhaps once formed a big
creature's bed. It seems that it has long been abandoned though, leaving it to
the pests infesting it and the decayed remains of the carcass which it bears.
The fallen cave opens up into a larger space to the west.
name: A Collapsed Cave under the Boulder

desc:
The dark earth tunnel opens up into a natural, underground stone cave. Bones
bearing teeth marks, and even whole skeletons, lie higgledy-piggedly in a huge
pile just below a narrow hole in one wall of the cave. The hole gives onto
another, smaller cave which floor is higher than that of the main cave, so that
it forms a sort of raised platform which has been covered with brittle twigs and
leaves. The hard stone floor is stained with large patches of dried-up blood,
combined with traces of tails, legs and decaying skulls.
name: A Bear's Den

desc:
Bones and corpses wrapped tightly in cocoons of web lie scattered carelessly
like rubbish or toys. The webs are thicker here than in the tunnel north, lining
utterly everything in a beautifully horrific manner. The surroundings of this
rancid dead end are so dense that no sunlight can possibly penetrate through.
name: Cluster of Cocoons

desc:
In this webbed, winding tunnel, there is just enough room to manoeuvre around a
massive, web-covered tree trunk that stands just off-centre in this cramped
space. The flooring here has been put together from branches and vines that are
held together with the help of strong, sticky cobwebs. A sticky web has been
woven across the tunnel to the east.
name: Bend around a Massive Trunk

desc:
About four feet separates the web-infested walls of this gently swaying abode.
One can discern that one of the walls is also the thick tree trunk that keeps
this passage in place. West of here, a small hourglass-shaped platform opens up.
It is also possible to move up or down along the tree-trunk, utilising a few
rough steps that have been cut into it.
name: Pocket of Air within the Webs

desc:
The blackness of the cobweb-covered walls in here are in stark contrast to the
large, corpse-white cocoons that have been stuck to the walls and ceilings. The
only exit out of here is to the east, into what appears to be a far darker and
even more cramped area. A strong stench of slime, stale air, and rot pervades
the air.
name: Foul-Smelling Breeding Area

desc:
Strange, thick, almost transparent cobwebs cover everything in here, and it
would be hard to know that this place is far high up in a tree top if it were
not for the gentle, almost lulling rocking of the gigantic canopy. South of
here, some sort of dark and foreboding lair can be seen, while a series of
bundled logs and boughs lead north. There is also the possibility of lowering
oneself into a dark hole that sits in the middle of this area.
name: Webbed Gangway

desc:
Twigs and branches have interwoven with each other, creating a natural nest of
sorts. The leaf-work is fairly intact, and by the look of the leaves one can
conclude that this gigantic tree is indeed an ancient oak. The footing is solid
and surprisingly even, perhaps too much so for being a creation of nature. To
the south, shiny silky cobwebs hang around a tunnel-like opening leading away
from this place.
name: Nest of Twigs and Branches

desc:
This small area of the treetops is neatly boxed-in on all sides except the north
where the ever-present webs continue. There is a range of small cocoons of
spider-silk here, made of small animals left here long ago. The remainder of the
cramped dead-end is lined with long webs, both old and new. The ground below is
hardly visible through gaps in web, leaf and bough.
name: Festering Cocoons

desc:
This place is an almost cave-like structure, made entirely from countless layers
of spider web. The temperature and humidity in here are higher than the
surrounding areas, and the air carries a foul, bitter smell. Along the walls,
open cocoons of varying sizes - up to four feet in diameter - have been meshed
to boughs and branches that stick out from the webs.
name: Den of Spiders

desc:
A plethora of plants, from common ivy and periwinkle to calluna and crimson
clover, grow here. The soggy soil appears to be an ideal place for the heavy
vegetation to grow. However, some of the trees have gaping, open wounds on their
trunks, and some of them have had their trunks cleaved down the middle. There is
a myriad of small bugs and animals nesting in the various bushes and protective
spaces created by the warped roots. The surroundings are extremely dense, and
together with the never ending darkness of Mirkwood, it makes it difficult to
scout anything but the immediate surroundings.
name: Deep in a Tangle

desc:
The air is damp and heavy in this part of Mirkwood. The forest floor consists of
mushy soil which is saturated with moisture. Snails, worms and other smaller
animals live off the decomposed, fallen vegetation. Spiders have spun their webs
between the twisting roots. The roots and vines compose a thick tangle, perfect
for setting traps or nesting. The intertwining tree crowns keep the forest in
perpetual darkness, making it difficult to discern much else but the immediate
surroundings.
name: Deep in the Mirkwood Tangle
